name: Bud Shank
abstract: Clifford Everett "Bud" Shank Jr. (May 27, 1926 – April 2, 2009) was an American alto saxophonist and flautist.

concerts.label: Stan Kenton: Innovations in Modern Music
Stan Kenton: Innovations in Modern Music

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Barnet
about_quotes.evidence: Bud worked for me several times, as a tenor man and an alto man, both.

about_quotes.speaker: Holman Bill
about_quotes.evidence: The James Dean Story. Chet Baker and Bud Shank, listed as the leaders on this one... You're credited with arranging... Leith Stevens had done the music for that picture. They were all his themes, and I just made arrangements out of them for a medium-sized jazz band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Holman Bill — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Scott
about_quotes.evidence: I had Vince DeRosa on French horn, you know Ray Brown played bass, you know Bud Shank on woodwinds and just you know, and the best violin players in the business.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Scott interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 1, 2022 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Strazzeri
about_quotes.evidence: I played with all these guys and strictly in a quartet vein, I played with Bud Shank or played with Frank Rosolino...
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Strazzeri interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Jolly
about_quotes.evidence: We had Art Farmer, Frank Rosolino, Bud Shank, Shelly, Red Mitchell, Gerry Mulligan and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Jolly interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: I had a job with Skinnay Ennis’ band, which was a real nice band, Bud Shank was in it
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
